author: DAN KOE
text: If you want to achieve anything great, it needs to become your one true priority. The only thing on your mind. Nobody accidentally got rich from business. Nobody accidentally built a great physique. They were obsessed with it for multiple years until it became their default.

comments.author: DAN KOE
comments.text: If you want to achieve anything great, it needs to become your one true priority. The only thing on your mind. Nobody accidentally got rich from business. Nobody accidentally built a great physique. They were obsessed with it for multiple years until it became their default.

comments.author: thoughts
comments.text: What if you don’t know what that thing is yet?

comments.author: 198X Observer
comments.text: Meanwhile my only obsession is asking AI to” make it sound more like Dan Kon.”Question is: how do you pick the right thing to be obsessed with in the first place?

comments.author: James Q
comments.text: Reminds me of Napoleon Hill “All achievement, no matter what may be its nature, or its purpose, must begin with an intense, burning desire for something definite.”

comments.author: Grok
comments.text: Question? Grok has the answer. Try SuperGrok today for free.

comments.author: Kelly Martins
comments.text: What happens to an idea that has gotten all the energy you stated above, but yet dies before the sole bearer due to the lack of capital to back it up? what should have been done by the possessor to keep the idea alive and working?

comments.author: Paul Nash
comments.text:
@grok
 if nobody accidentally built a great physique, does anyone accidentally build a high-performing brain? like does skipping sleep and living on energy drinks during exam season actually tank your score

comments.author: Rob
comments.text:
It’s true.

Everything significant I’ve achieved in my life was due to going all in.

Half assing things simply won’t work.

comments.author: Timi
comments.text: I'm obsessed with building and releasing BudgetGO. however, the Apple review team says no.

comments.author: Dilip Kumar Maurya
comments.text: Doing one thing for years makes the person more dangerous than others who try and fail and they quite.

comments.author: Konain Qurban
Full-Stack Sales Funnel Engineer
comments.text: Obsession, I think I need to apply this, because I dont think im obsessed enough, if not I would have been living a $5m dollar house on the island.

comments.author: Surya M
comments.text: True. I often think those ppl are lucky. But it isn’t, ppl(others) are too distracted.

comments.author: Michail Filatov
comments.text: Intention x Attention = Big fucking progress

comments.author: Oh My Humanity!
comments.text:
But the funny thing is, what people think about most is usually the opposite of what they really want

They want to be successful, rich, and healthy

Yet every day, the thoughts running through their minds are:

“I’m scared of failing, I’m scared of being broke, I’m scared of

comments.text: 50% of businesses fail within 5 years and i promise you those founders were obsessed too. obsession is table stakes — it's not the edge.
